quote_of_day.text:
>>3566
Mai achcha karu, toh bhi bura hi hota hai mere saath.(No matter what Good I do, always something bad happens to me)

Also, this is a inclusive space for our southie pajeets we should talk in English for most part.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of nostalgia sat over the board this morning, with Anon reflecting on 'gems of timmy culture'. This brought scattered showers of 2000s pop culture references throughout the day.

A front of geopolitical tension moved in during the afternoon, as Anon sparked discussion on 'jeets' and passports. This drew a flurry of responses and some intense debate.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of personal details occurred when one Anon shared their age, height, and job, sparking curiosity among other users.

A cold snap of skepticism hit the board in the evening, as Anon sought confirmation from self-described 'chads' on a particular claim.

Elsewhere, a gentle breeze of fatalism blew through with the phrase 'Kar bura toh ho bhala', which seemed to spark little controversy.

The overall climate remained clear, with users freely expressing their thoughts across various topics.

articles.title: Anon muses on 'gems of timmy culture'
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion with a post reading 'Gems of timmy culture.' One respondent later wrote that a previous poster was 'such an Uncle Tom' and questioned whether whites were of any help, adding that the only good thing about them was some of their technology.

Another anon reacted to a post with 'Yaar gobarputra, fuck off to your gobar womb.' A different poster called someone 'gobarputra' and another referred to someone as 'gobarmuncher.' One participant referenced 'Aryans' and was told to 'stop hating Aryans' by another anon who addressed them as 'dalit.' The discussion included several brief exchanges, with one poster writing 'Kek yeh toh haggu nikla' in response to another.

A user also asked 'Can you blame her tho?' in reference to an unspecified situation. The conversation featured several instances of insults and personal attacks, with one poster telling another to 'fuck off.'

articles.title: Anon Sparks Discussion on Jeets and Passports
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying 'Jeets here complicate any thing'. One anon responded with 'Ab kya hua ??'. Another anon mentioned they were having trouble getting a passport, saying 'Passport na ban raha pajeetia mai, ek mahine se try kar raha'.

A respondent suggested the passport issue might not be the only problem, stating '>>3542
because they have invaders rape genes. Their randi slut women are made for hindu bdl anyways'. Another anon chimed in, saying '>>3501
> IP change
HOW?'.

A respondent replied, '>pajeet doesn't know what dynamic IPs are'. The conversation turned to comparisons between India and Pakistan, with one anon saying 'Pakistan is much better thn Pajeetia'. Some anons engaged in personal attacks.

One claimed 'dalits have 5000 year of oppression depression genes'. A respondent shot back, '>>3559
Aa gaya aukat pe dalit'.

The discussion remained confrontational, with one anon telling another, '>>3542
Toh jaa na, ani natnl vermin', and another saying '>>3499
Then go to Pakistan, you anti-natalist'. A respondent ended with '>>3551
Tu visa dilwa de'.

articles.title: User provides personal details, sparks curiosity
articles.body:
Anon posted a brief personal profile, stating they are 23 years old, 5'8", and work as a journalist. One anon questioned whether an LLM was used to generate the post, referencing other post IDs.

Another anon asked about the gender of the original poster. A respondent made a comment in Hindi, which translates to "So what do we do, sister fucker?" One user jokingly compared the original poster to "obcanon".

Anon also used terms like "aromatic asexual" and "aromantic asexual" when describing themselves or in response to the original post.

The original post drew various reactions, including a request to rate something and a claim that a provided image was real.

articles.title: Anon seeks confirmation from 'chads'
articles.body: Anon posted 'Can any chad confirm this???' Another anon replied that there are no 'chads' on the board, stating they left for other platforms like Twitter and Instagram. A respondent joked about 'belting randijeetas' being a fundamental right of 'jeetcels'. One anon simply stated 'Industroon is dead'.

articles.title: Anon kicks off with Kar bura toh ho bhala
articles.body:
Anon started the thread with 'Kar bura toh ho bhala'. One respondent shared a similar sentiment, saying no matter what good they do, something bad always happens to them.

Another anon suggested inviting Pakistanis to the board, claiming they have more in common with them than with South Indians. One user joked about getting Pakistanis on board to see 'Pajeet crying for real'.

The conversation took a turn with an anon claiming to have posted all baits on an international board.
